meta.topic: A / an, the and zero article 2

step_1_context.text: We use a / an to talk about a type of job:

step_2_explanation.simple_rule:
We use a / an to talk about a type of job:
 • She was a company director when she retired.
step_2_explanation.formula: We usually use zero article before the name of an individual person or place.
step_2_explanation.common_mistake:
Note that a / an, or sometimes zero article, is used with a name when referring to the particular excellent qualities of the person named:
 • Majid plays tennis well, but he'll never be (a) Roger Federer.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 46.1.1 She was determined to be author one day. (Find the mistake).
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Use 'a/an' to talk about a type of job.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 46.1.2 I didn't even know Clara was interested in art until I heard that she owns Van Gogh.
options: owns Van Gogh -> owns a Van Gogh
I didn't even know -> I hadn't even known
step_3_interactive_exercises.correct_answer: owns Van Gogh -> owns a Van Gogh
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Use 'a' / 'an' for a work by a particular artist.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 46.2.1 I'm ___ marketing adviser at Dozzle.
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Use 'a' for a type of job.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 46.2.2 She has been appointed ___ Minister for Industry. (Give all possible answers).
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: For a unique position/title, use 'the' or zero article.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 46.3.1 Rodrigo's boat wasn't the most elegant in the harbour, but it was certainly ___ biggest. Can we omit 'the'?
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: When there is no noun or defining phrase after the superlative, 'the' can often be left out.
